name: Orange Marmalade Cake
recipeInstructions:
Preparation time: 10 minutes. Baking time: 55 to 60 minutes. Oven temperature: 350 degrees F.
This is a nutty, old-fashioned spice cake traditional in the South. When made with raspberry or blackberry jam, it's called "Jam Cake". For one Bundt cake serving 10 to 12 servings:
Vanilla wafer crumbs Sliced oranges, for garnish
Caramel Frosting: Combine 3/4 cup light brown sugar, 1/4 cup whipping cream, 2 teaspoons dark corn syrup, and a pinch of salt in a saucepan. Add 2 tablespoons butter. Bring to a boil. Boil until the syrup reaches 234 degrees F on a candy thermometer or until a drop in cold water forms a soft ball. Beat until cooled and creamy.
1. In the large bowl of an electric mixer, combine the flour, sugar, cinnamon, baking powder, salt, cloves, nutmeg, butter, marmalade, eggs, milk, lemon juice, and nuts.
2. Blend at low speed, scraping the bowl. Increase speed to medium and beat for 2 minutes.
3. Butter an 8 1/2-inch (10-cup) tube pan. Dust with vanilla wafer crumbs.
4. Pour batter into the prepared pan.
5. Bake at 350 degrees F for 55 minutes or until a skewer inserted in the center comes out clean.
6. Remove from the oven and cool for 5 minutes.
7. Turn out of the pan and finish cooling on a rack.
8. Glaze with Caramel Frosting and garnish with sliced fruit.
